description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who maintains a small command-line utility stored in
/home/user/finops-tool.  
A new patch release is required.  
Your goal is to bump the project’s semantic version **from 2.1.4 to 2.1.5** and to record the
change in the project changelog.  
The work is limited to *exactly* the three files listed below; no other files or directories
should be modified or created except the explicitly requested log file.

Files that already exist before you start:
1. /home/user/finops-tool/version.txt  
   • Contains just one line with the current version string:  
     2.1.4
2. /home/user/finops-tool/CHANGELOG.md  
   • Begins with a Markdown H1 header “# Changelog” followed by the two most recent
     release notes, the first of which is the 2.1.4 section.  
     (The exact initial contents are set up for you; you do NOT need to recreate them.)

Actions you must complete:
A. Update /home/user/finops-tool/version.txt so that its single line now reads:  
   2.1.5

B. Prepend a new section *at the very top* of /home/user/finops-tool/CHANGELOG.md (immediately
after the “# Changelog” header and a blank line) with the exact block shown below.
The automated tests are strict about both wording and punctuation; copy it verbatim:

## [2.1.5] - 2023-10-05
### Changed
- Optimized storage tiering algorithm leading to 12% cost savings (#41)

C. Create a plain-text log file called /home/user/finops-tool/bump.log that will be used by
auditors.  The file must contain **exactly two lines** in this order:

old_version=2.1.4
new_version=2.1.5

No extra spaces or newline characters are allowed before, after, or between the lines.

Success criteria that the grading script will check:
1. /home/user/finops-tool/version.txt consists of the single line “2.1.5”.
2. /home/user/finops-tool/CHANGELOG.md starts with “# Changelog”, followed by a blank line,
   followed by the three-line 2.1.5 section shown above, followed by the pre-existing 2.1.4
   section exactly as it was.
3. /home/user/finops-tool/bump.log exists and its contents match the two-line format specified
   in step C.

You do not need more than two or three shell commands to accomplish all of this.
truth:
Initial state (before agent starts):
1. The directory /home/user/finops-tool already exists with 755 permissions.
2. /home/user/finops-tool/version.txt (644) initially contains:
   2.1.4\n
3. /home/user/finops-tool/CHANGELOG.md (644) initially contains exactly:

# Changelog

## [2.1.4] - 2023-07-15
### Fixed
- Fixed tagging bug in cost explorer sync (#32)

## [2.1.3] - 2023-06-10
### Added
- Added forecast cost anomaly detection (#28)

4. No /home/user/finops-tool/bump.log file exists yet.

Expected final state after correct completion:
1. /home/user/finops-tool/version.txt now contains exactly:
   2.1.5\n
2. /home/user/finops-tool/CHANGELOG.md now contains exactly:

# Changelog

## [2.1.5] - 2023-10-05
### Changed
- Optimized storage tiering algorithm leading to 12% cost savings (#41)

## [2.1.4] - 2023-07-15
### Fixed
- Fixed tagging bug in cost explorer sync (#32)

## [2.1.3] - 2023-06-10
### Added
- Added forecast cost anomaly detection (#28)

(There is a single blank line after “# Changelog” and after each section; other whitespace is exactly as shown.)

3. /home/user/finops-tool/bump.log (644) exists with exactly:
old_version=2.1.4\nnew_version=2.1.5

4. No other files or directories were created or modified.
